Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRISCOE IVESTER & BAZEL LLP's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS:

Briscoe Ivester & Bazel, LLP's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to defendants' cross-complaints is granted without leave to amend. The fundamental basis of declaratory relief is the existence of an actual, present controversy over a proper subject. (Linda Vista Village San Diego Homeowners Assn., Inc. v. Tecolote Investors, LLC (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 166, 181; Osseous Technology of America, Inc. v. DiscoveryOrtho Partners LLC (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 357, 367 ["There is unanimity of authority to the effect that the declaratory procedure operates prospectively, and not merely for the redress of past wrongs"].) A trial court may decline to exercise its power to grant declaratory relief whenever a declaration is not necessary or proper under all the circumstances. (Code Civ. Proc., section 1061; TransparentGov Novato v. City of Novato (2019) 34 Cal.App.5th 140, 148.) Where, as here, "[n]o facts are alleged which would render necessary or proper a declaration with respect to the future conduct of the parties," declaratory relief is unwarranted. (Travers v. Louden (1967) 254 Cal.App.2d 926, 929; accord, Osseous Technologies of America, Inc., 191 Cal.App.4th at 360, 366-375 [court did not abuse its discretion in sustaining demurrer to declaratory relief complaint where "the facts as pleaded amounted to a breach of contract dispute (looking to past conduct) rather than a forward-looking pronouncement of the rights and duties of the parties"].) Defendants' cross-complaints do not relate to the future conduct of the parties. Further, the factual and other issues they raise look to past conduct, and will be resolved in the existing action, either on summary judgment or at trial. Finally, the court has already decided defendants' claim in the purported fourth cause of action of the cross-complaints that the agreement between the parties did not comply with Business & Professions Code section 6148.

order is signed.

Off Calendar Apr-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert White and Susan Rushakoff, counsel for Plaintiff, Briscoe Ivester & Bazel LLP; David Commins and Eric Wallis, counsel for Defendants Security National Properties and related entities.

The Court recites the general history of the proceedings, confirms that the case has not been assigned to this department for all purposes, and notes that the apparent outstanding issue at this time concerns discovery as to jurisdictional issues.

The Court reviews the Joint Report, noting the areas of agreement and requests counsel to prepare an Order reflecting those areas of agreement on pages one and two of the Joint Report.

With respect to the items of disagreement, the Court notes that since it perceives the issues at this time to be limited to jurisdictional issues, the scope of the discovery would be not as broad as general discovery. However, to the extent discovery is propounded and to which there are objections, any resulting motions would be directed to Dept. 302 for resolution.

Mr. White raises some concern with the ostensibly limited scope of the discovery since the alter ego allegations would, in his view, require an arguably broader scope. Mr. Wallis responds. The Court instructs counsel to prepare an Order memorializing the terms of the agreed matters and to otherwise commence discovery under the parameters noted and pursue relief in Dept. 302 as necessary.

Off Calendar Aug-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Security National Master Holding Company, Llc, Security National, Inc., Race Investments, Llc, Sn Commercial, Llc, Sn Servicing Corporation And Security National Properties Holding Company, Llc's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is OFF CALENDAR without prejudice to re-notice and re-setting. As plaintiff requests, and defendants concede, this Court may - and hereby does - STAY this motion in order to permit discovery on the jurisdictional issues. (See Goehring v. Superior Court (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 894, 911; HealthMarkets, Inc. v. Superior Court (2009) 1160, 1173.) Lead counsel - Mr. White and Mr. Abelson - are to meet and confer regarding the timing and parameters of the jurisdictional discovery. The meet-and-confer is to be conducted face-to-face within the next 21 calendar days at Mr. White's office and will last a minimum of 30 minutes. Subject matters shall include: topics of discovery, length of the discovery period, discovery devices, and any time or numerical limits on discovery devices. Following the meet-and-confer, the parties are to file and set for hearing a joint report. The first pages of this joint report are to clearly and concisely state what the parties agree upon, the next pages are to state plaintiff's position on issues the parties do not agree upon, then defendants are to state their positions on non-agreed issues. The report is to be no more than 10 pages in length - double-spaced, 28-line pages, 12-point Times New Roman type. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
